testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Fix production build and client/server module boundaries
userStories.description:
Production build currently fails because Node/server-only modules are reachable from the browser graph. Fix this first so bundle analysis and later frontend performance work can be measured reliably.

Context:
- `pnpm build` fails in `src/lib/xdg.ts` because browser bundling sees Node modules like `os`, `path`, and `fs`.
- Vite also warns that many API/lib modules pull Node modules into client resolution.
- Likely causes include top-level server-only imports from `src/api/*`, `src/lib/logger.ts`, `src/lib/xdg.ts`, `src/lib/db.ts`, and broad frontend barrels/imports.

Implementation notes:
- Keep client-callable `createServerFn` entry modules thin and move Node/database work behind server-only implementation modules.
- Avoid top-level imports of Node built-ins from files reachable by route/component imports.
- Extend the existing Vite server-only guard if useful, but prefer fixing import boundaries over adding stubs.
- Preserve existing API behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm build` completes successfully.
- Production client build no longer reports Node built-in externalization warnings for reachable frontend modules.
- Browser/client code cannot import `src/lib/xdg.ts`, `src/lib/logger.ts`, `src/lib/db.ts`, migration/backup/lockfile/watcher/integrity modules, or other server-only implementation files directly.
- Existing tests for server functions and frontend still pass or are updated for the new boundaries.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Add frontend bundle analysis and performance guardrails
userStories.description:
Add a repeatable way to measure frontend bundle output after the production build is fixed, then capture a baseline for future optimization work.

Context:
- The current build failed before chunk stats were available.
- `vite.config.ts` has no explicit bundle analysis script, visualizer, chunk budget, or documented performance baseline.

Implementation notes:
- Add a bundle analysis workflow that works with the current Vite/TanStack Start setup.
- Prefer a script that can be run locally and in CI without requiring a browser.
- Capture chunk sizes and identify heavy shared dependencies such as charting, DnD, form, devtools, and server-fn client shims.
- Add documentation or a short report file with the baseline and how to regenerate it.

Acceptance criteria:
- A script exists to build and inspect production client bundle output.
- The baseline identifies top chunks/modules and initial-route payload risks.
- Bundle analysis can run after `pnpm build` without manual steps.
- Reasonable chunk warning/budget guidance is documented.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Enable route-level code splitting for TanStack Router routes
userStories.description:
Reduce initial JavaScript by ensuring route modules are split and loaded only when needed.

Context:
- `src/routeTree.gen.ts` statically imports every route module.
- No lazy route files or lazy route components were found.
- This can pull board DnD, dashboard charts, ticket detail, epic detail, and project/dev tooling into early chunks.

Implementation notes:
- Use the TanStack Router/Start-supported route splitting approach for this project.
- Regenerate route tree as needed.
- Keep route loaders and head metadata working.
- Confirm pending/error components still behave correctly.
- Do not manually edit generated route tree except through the supported generator/plugin configuration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Initial route no longer eagerly imports every route component module.
- Heavy route surfaces (`/board`, `/dashboard`, `/ticket/$id`, `/epic/$id`, `/projects/$projectId`) are split into lazy route chunks or equivalent.
- Route navigation still works for all routes.
- Bundle analysis shows route-level chunks rather than one monolithic app chunk.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Lazy-load root layout modals and development-only tooling
userStories.description:
Shrink the root layout client graph by loading rarely used UI only when users open it, and keep devtools out of production initial load.

Context:
- `src/routes/__root.tsx` imports and renders TanStack devtools unconditionally.
- `src/components/AppLayout.tsx` statically imports many modal/tooling components, including ticket/project/epic/settings/feedback/import/logs/inception/shortcuts flows.
- `ImportModal` and `InceptionModal` are mounted even when closed.

Implementation notes:
- Dynamically import TanStack devtools only in development.
- Use `React.lazy`/`Suspense` or route-compatible lazy imports for rarely used modals and heavy panels.
- Render `ImportModal` and `InceptionModal` only when open.
- Keep accessible modal loading fallbacks minimal and non-disruptive.
- Avoid introducing global loading spinners for ordinary page rendering.

Acceptance criteria:
- Production initial layout does not include TanStack devtools UI code.
- Root layout no longer statically imports rarely-used modal implementations.
- Closed modals are not mounted.
- Opening each lazy modal still works and preserves existing behavior.
- Bundle analysis shows reduced root/initial chunk size.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Split dashboard chart bundles and remove heavy barrel imports
userStories.description:
Keep dashboard charting dependencies out of routes/tabs that do not need them, and preserve lazy loading for Recharts/ECharts-heavy surfaces.

Context:
- `src/routes/dashboard.tsx` imports dashboard components from `src/components/dashboard/index.ts`.
- The dashboard barrel re-exports chart-heavy modules including Recharts and ECharts surfaces.
- `AnalyticsSection` already lazy-loads several Recharts components, but barrel imports can pull modules back into the dashboard route chunk.
- `AITelemetryTab` directly imports Recharts and `CostExplorerTab` lazy-loads ECharts.

Implementation notes:
- Replace dashboard route barrel imports with direct imports for only the components used immediately.
- Avoid exporting heavy chart modules from broad barrels that route modules import.
- Lazy-load secondary dashboard tabs where appropriate, especially AI telemetry and cost explorer chart surfaces.
- Keep hover/intent prefetch for cost explorer or add equivalent for telemetry.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dashboard route no longer imports from a barrel that re-exports all chart modules.
- Recharts and ECharts code are isolated to lazy chunks or tab-specific chunks.
- Overview renders correctly and secondary tabs load correctly.
- Bundle analysis shows chart libraries are not part of unrelated initial chunks.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Improve route loaders and navigation cache reuse
userStories.description:
Make TanStack Router loaders and TanStack Query work together so navigation renders with cached data instead of loading gaps and duplicate requests.

Context:
- Dashboard, board, list, and epic loaders fire `void ensureQueryData(...)` and return immediately, so route pending components do not wait for critical data.
- Ticket detail route lacks a loader and uses a component query with `staleTime: 0`.
- Board/list modal opens call `getTicket` directly instead of using Query cache.

Implementation notes:
- Await above-the-fold route data in loaders, while leaving secondary tabs/analytics as background prefetch or hover/intent prefetch.
- Add a ticket detail route loader with `ensureQueryData(queryKeys.ticket(id))`.
- Update board/list ticket modal open flows and search-result selection to use `queryClient.ensureQueryData` with `queryKeys.ticket(id)`.
- Use practical `staleTime` values and targeted invalidation so recently loaded detail data is reused.

Acceptance criteria:
- Critical route data is awaited where it affects first paint.
- Ticket detail navigation reuses cached detail data when available.
- Opening the same ticket from board/list does not trigger redundant uncached fetches.
- Secondary dashboard tab data is prefetched only on intent or after the critical view.
- Existing pending/error states still behave correctly.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Tighten TanStack Query keys, invalidation, polling, and search
userStories.description:
Reduce unnecessary network activity and observer churn by aligning query keys, invalidation, polling, and search with TanStack Query best practices.

Context:
- `queryKeys.allTickets = ["tickets"]` also prefixes count queries like `projectTicketCounts` and `epicTicketCounts`.
- Several mutation invalidations are broad and may invalidate more than intended.
- `useSearch` manually manages debounce/results instead of using existing `queryKeys.search`.
- Active Ralph session polling is observed from multiple always-mounted places.
- Comments polling can combine interval and window-focus refetches.
- Cost explorer summary mounts a second observer for related data.

Implementation notes:
- Move count keys to a separate namespace or use exact invalidation where appropriate.
- Convert search to debounced `useQuery` with `enabled`, `staleTime`, and race-safe behavior.
- Centralize active Ralph session polling or expose it through layout/context to avoid redundant observers.
- Tune polling hooks to avoid refetch-on-focus storms when intervals are active.
- Reduce duplicate observers such as cost explorer summary where summary can be derived locally.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ticket list invalidations no longer accidentally target count queries unless intended.
- Search results are cached per query/project and cannot be overwritten by stale older requests.
- Active Ralph polling behavior is centralized or clearly deduplicated.
- Polling hooks avoid extra focus refetches when interval polling is enabled.
- Cost explorer no longer mounts duplicate observers for the same data unless justified.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Split AppLayout state to reduce global rerenders
userStories.description:
Reduce rerenders caused by the broad `AppLayout` context by splitting state into smaller, stable contexts or an equivalent selector-based store.

Context:
- `AppLayout` exposes filters, modal state/actions, refresh state, search selection, sample data state, epic deletion, mobile menu state, and projects panel state in one context.
- Consumers that only need one action can rerender when unrelated state changes.
- The layout also owns several polling-driven values that can fan out through context.

Implementation notes:
- Split into focused contexts such as filters, modal actions/state, refresh/search, mobile/projects panel, and sample-data/epic deletion.
- Prefer stable action-only contexts where possible.
- Keep public hooks ergonomic and update consumers incrementally.
- Avoid overengineering; make the smallest split that isolates high-frequency state changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unrelated context consumers no longer rerender for filter/modal/refresh/search/mobile state changes they do not use.
- Existing keyboard shortcuts, modals, filtering, mobile menu, and projects panel behavior still works.
- Tests cover representative consumers or existing tests are updated.
- React profiler/manual verification shows reduced rerender fanout for common actions such as search, filter change, modal open, and refresh.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Optimize Kanban and list rendering for large ticket sets
userStories.description:
Improve render performance for large boards and lists by reducing per-ticket prop churn, unnecessary allocations, and all-card rendering work.

Context:
- `KanbanBoard` renders every sortable card in every column.
- `SortableContext.items` allocates arrays during render.
- Focus state changes can update props across all cards.
- Board route passes callbacks that are recreated each render.
- Per-card Ralph session lookup runs during every board render.
- `TicketListView` already uses virtualization but still formats dates and creates row callbacks during render.

Implementation notes:
- Wrap board route handlers in `useCallback` and memoize `allEpics`.
- Memoize per-column ticket IDs and reduce per-card prop churn.
- Consider per-column virtualization or a threshold-based compact mode for very large columns.
- Isolate keyboard focus state so only previous/new focused cards need visual updates where feasible.
- Pass memoized active-session data rather than invoking lookups for each card on each render.
- Precompute list row dates/parsed metadata and fix virtualization spacer row structure if needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Board interactions remain accessible and DnD still works.
- Large columns render fewer unnecessary updates or use a documented threshold strategy.
- Common board state changes do not rerender every card unless ticket data actually changes.
- List virtualization remains correct and table layout is valid.
- Existing board/list tests pass and new coverage is added for any changed behavior.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Audit and tune TanStack Form modal performance
userStories.description:
Review form-heavy modal surfaces for TanStack Form best practices, field subscription granularity, and unnecessary rerenders after lazy-loading makes them load on demand.

Context:
- Ticket, epic, settings, and project flows use TanStack Form or form-like local state.
- `TicketModal` already has comments about subscribing to specific values, but form-heavy modals are still part of core user flows.
- Once modals are lazy-loaded, their runtime render behavior should also be checked.

Implementation notes:
- Audit `TicketModal`, `EpicModal`, `SettingsModal`, project/import forms, and nested ticket form components.
- Prefer field-level subscriptions/selectors over broad form state subscriptions.
- Avoid copying server/query data into form state before data is guaranteed loaded.
- Ensure submit buttons are disabled during pending mutations and mutation error states are surfaced.
- Keep server state and form state boundaries clear.

Acceptance criteria:
- Form components subscribe only to state they render or need for submit behavior.
- Create/edit forms prevent double submits and show mutation errors consistently.
- Existing default values are initialized only after required query/server data is available.
- No form behavior regresses for ticket, epic, project, settings, or import flows.
- Relevant tests are updated or added.
- Run `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

userStories.title: Validate frontend performance improvements and document results
userStories.description:
Run final measurement after the optimization tickets land, compare against the baseline, and document remaining risks or follow-up work.

Context:
- Earlier tickets should fix build output, split bundles, improve navigation caching, and reduce render churn.
- The epic needs a clear before/after summary so future work can tell whether changes materially improved load/navigation performance.

Implementation notes:
- Re-run the bundle analysis from the baseline ticket.
- Capture production build chunk sizes and notable heavy dependencies.
- Exercise key user flows: initial project list load, board navigation, list navigation, dashboard tabs, ticket modal open, ticket detail route, settings/modal open.
- Use existing profiler/navigation timing helpers where practical.
- Document follow-up items that remain too large for this epic.

Acceptance criteria:
- A before/after summary exists with bundle size and route/navigation observations.
- Core flows are manually verified or covered by automated checks where available.
- Any remaining performance risks are documented as follow-up tickets or notes.
- The final state passes `pnpm type-check && pnpm lint && pnpm test && pnpm build`.

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription:
Improve Brain Dump frontend performance from production build stability through initial load, route navigation, TanStack Query usage, and React render efficiency.

Primary goals:
- Make the production frontend build pass reliably.
- Keep server-only Node/database modules out of the browser graph.
- Reduce initial JavaScript by route-splitting and lazy-loading rarely used UI.
- Improve navigation by using loaders and Query cache consistently.
- Reduce rerenders in layout, board, list, and polling-heavy paths.
- Add measurement guardrails so future regressions are visible.
